mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 7.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high UNRATE regimes exceed 0.5% in 60% of cases or Rank IC > 0.05 in 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) combined with low rates (FEDFUNDS < 1%) creates a 'stagflation' risk premium, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in stagflation regimes are positive in > 50% of cases or interaction effect size < 0.1 in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO < 98.5) signals weakening supply-side dynamics, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in INDPRO decline regimes exceed 0.3% in 70% of cases or Rank IC < 0.02 in 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS > 135k) reflects strong labor market demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in PAYEMS growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.1) or Rank IC < 0.04 in 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in housing starts (HOUST > 850k) signals strong demand for construction, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in HOUST growth regimes are negative in > 40% of cases or Rank IC < 0.03 in 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1 > 2%) and retail sales (RSAFS > 420k) indicates robust demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in demand growth regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect size < 0.15 in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in the federal funds rate above 1% signals monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in FEDFUNDS rise regimes exceed -0.2% in 60% of cases or Rank IC > 0.04 in 3-year windows.
